sections.heading: GovSecure Third-Party AI Privacy Risk Assessment
paragraphs: Vendor Questionnaire and Evidence Review Pack

sections.heading: Purpose
paragraphs: Assess privacy, data protection, governance, and AI data-use risk in third-party AI vendors, deployers, and adopters — with a primary emphasis on vendor accountability.
Designed for use during onboarding, procurement review, renewal review, major product changes, and post-incident reassessment.
This document includes section numbering, answer fields, evidence-request columns, reviewer risk scoring, red-flag triggers, and an executive summary page for internal reviewers.

sections.heading: Executive Summary for Reviewers
paragraphs: Complete after receiving the vendor response and evidence pack.

bullets: 4 = High concern / mitigation or leadership review needed
5 = Critical concern / automatic escalation if unresolved
sections.heading: Risk Scoring Guidance

bullets: Customer data is used for training, tuning, or product improvement by default.
Vendor cannot explain retention, deletion scope, or storage locations.
No privacy lead, no privacy program, or no DPA support.
Cross-tenant exposure is possible or not clearly ruled out.
Sensitive or regulated data is permitted without explicit safeguards.
Broad human review occurs without strict controls.
Subprocessors or transfer mechanisms are not disclosed.
Recent privacy or security incident lacks clear remediation.
sections.heading: Automatic Red-Flag Triggers

bullets: Vendor response: complete the 'Answer Field' area for each question or provide a linked workbook response.
Evidence requested: attach or reference the supporting artifact where available.
Reviewer notes / risk lens: reserved for GovSecure or internal reviewers after submission.
Score: reviewer enters 1–5 after evaluating the response and evidence.
Red-flag trigger: indicates conditions that should automatically trigger escalation or conditional approval.
sections.heading: How to Complete This Questionnaire
paragraphs: Suggested response types: Yes / No; Yes / No / Partial; Select all that apply; Short answer; Long answer; Attachment required; Date.

sections.heading: Section 1 — Vendor / Provider Profile
tables: Company registration or legal entity record
No legal entity or ambiguous provider identity
Product being assessed is not clearly identified
Public product page or documentation link
Headquarters or legal jurisdiction cannot be confirmed
Countries where service is delivered or supported
Service region list or support matrix
Material operations occur in high-risk or unknown jurisdictions
Primary business contact name and title
Privacy office or DPO contact details
No privacy contact or escalation channel
No security contact or escalation channel

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Role statement (provider, deployer, integrator, processor, controller, etc.)
Vendor cannot clearly define its legal or operational role
Briefly describe the AI product or service being assessed
Product overview, architecture summary, or model card
Product scope, intended purpose, or AI functionality is unclear
Describe intended business use case(s) for the customer
Use case summary or implementation scope
Use case is open-ended, high impact, or not bounded

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Capability description (GenAI, ML, recommendation, agentic, biometric, profiling, etc.)
High-risk capability is used without supporting controls

sections.heading: Section 2 — AI System Overview
tables: What type of AI model(s) or services are used?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Model architecture summary or model list
Model stack or provider dependencies are not disclosed
Identify all third-party model providers, cloud providers, subprocessors, or data partners involved
Architecture diagram and third-party dependency list
Critical dependencies are withheld or materially incomplete
Does the solution process customer inputs in real time, store them, or both?
Data flow diagram or processing description
Storage or processing behavior is unclear
Does the solution use customer data for inference only, product improvement, model fine-tuning, training, analytics, or human review?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Data use statement and contract terms
Customer data is used for training or improvement by default
Does the AI system make decisions, recommendations, or outputs that could materially affect individuals?
High-impact decision support exists without extra safeguards
If yes, describe the types of impact
Impact assessment or use case classification
Potential effect on employment, access, finance, health, or rights is not evaluated
Is human review required before high-impact outputs or actions are used?
No human review for high-impact outputs or actions
Can the system take actions automatically on behalf of a user or organization?
Autonomous actions exist without scoping or approval thresholds

sections.heading: Section 3 — Personal Data and Privacy Scope
tables: What categories of personal data may be processed by the service?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Data inventory or processing category list
Extensive personal data categories are processed without minimization
What categories of sensitive or regulated data may be processed?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Sensitive data inventory or control statement
Sensitive or regulated data is permitted without explicit safeguards
Is the customer permitted to submit sensitive personal data into the service?
Sensitive data submission is allowed by default
If yes, what controls exist to restrict, mask, filter, classify, or protect that data?
DLP, filtering, classification, masking, or policy evidence
No preventive or detective controls for sensitive data
Does the system collect personal data directly from users, indirectly from customers, or from third-party sources?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Data source paths are not documented
Does the service infer new personal data, traits, profiles, or behavioral insights from submitted data?
Vendor creates inferred profiles without transparency or controls
Does the solution generate content that may contain personal data from training data, retrieval data, or prior prompts?
Potential regeneration of personal data is not tested or addressed
Can the solution return data belonging to another customer, tenant, or user?
Tenant isolation design and testing evidence
Cross-tenant leakage is possible or not ruled out
Describe all data elements required to use the service safely and effectively
Describe any data elements that are optional but commonly submitted by customers
Optional data includes unnecessary or sensitive content

sections.heading: Section 4 — Purpose Limitation and Data Use
tables: Describe the specific purposes for which customer data is processed
Purposes are broad, vague, or not limited
Is customer data used only to deliver the contracted service?
Customer data is used beyond contracted service delivery
Is customer data ever used for training, model improvement, benchmarking, analytics, advertising, profiling, research, or sharing with third parties?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Privacy terms and data use statement
Secondary data use exists without explicit opt-in
Can customers opt out of model training, product improvement, or analytics use of their data?
Admin settings, DPA clause, or product controls
No opt-out for non-essential secondary uses
Are opt-out settings enabled by default or only upon request?
If data is de-identified or anonymized, describe the method and whether re-identification risk is assessed
Are prompts, uploads, outputs, embeddings, logs, or telemetry reused for any secondary purpose?
Prompts or outputs are reused without customer awareness
If yes, describe each secondary purpose clearly
Secondary purpose is ambiguous or materially broad

sections.heading: Section 5 — Data Retention, Deletion, and Disposal
tables: What is the default retention period for prompts, uploads, outputs, histories, embeddings, logs, metadata, and human review content?
Retention periods are not defined or are excessive
Admin guide or product settings evidence
No configurable retention for customer-controlled data
Can customers delete submitted data, prompts, outputs, and logs on demand?
Customer deletion rights are not supported
When data is deleted, is it removed from production, backups, indexes, vector stores, training datasets, fine-tunes, and logs?
Describe deletion timelines and any exceptions
Deletion timing is unclear or materially delayed
If customer data is used in training or fine-tuning, can that data later be removed or unlearned?
No removal path for trained-on customer data
Describe secure disposal procedures at end of contract

sections.heading: Section 6 — Data Sharing, Subprocessors, and Cross-Border Transfers
tables: List all subprocessors involved in hosting, inference, storage, support, analytics, review, or model operations
Do subprocessors receive personal data, prompts, outputs, or metadata?
Data flow diagram or subprocessor data map
Vendor cannot specify what subprocessors receive
Are subprocessors contractually restricted from using customer data for their own purposes?
In which countries is customer data stored, processed, accessed, or supported?
Data residency and support location statement
Storage or access locations are unknown
Transfer map or regional processing statement
If yes, what transfer mechanism is used?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
SCCs, adequacy, UK IDTA, DPF, or equivalent evidence
No lawful transfer mechanism for restricted transfers
Can customers choose data residency or processing region?
Regional hosting options or contract term
No regional choice for sensitive workloads
Can vendor support personnel access customer data from outside the chosen region?
Remote support access bypasses region commitments
Describe controls for international transfers and remote support access
Support access is broad, uncontrolled, or unlogged

sections.heading: Section 7 — Privacy Governance and Accountability
tables: Do you maintain a documented privacy program?
Do you have a designated privacy officer, DPO, or equivalent accountable leader?
Org chart or privacy governance statement
Do you conduct privacy impact assessments, DPIAs, or AI-specific risk assessments for the product?
No privacy impact assessment for material AI processing
Is privacy-by-design formally incorporated into product development?
SDLC control or design review evidence
Are AI product changes subject to privacy review before release?
Material AI changes bypass privacy review
Do you maintain records of processing activities relevant to this service?
Do you have documented policies for privacy, retention, access control, AI governance, training data governance, data subject rights, and incident response?
Core governance policies are missing or immature
Are employees trained on privacy and responsible AI requirements?
Training completion summary or program description
No workforce training on privacy and responsible AI

sections.heading: Section 8 — Data Subject Rights and User Transparency
tables: Can customers fulfill data subject rights requests using your service?
Can personal data be located, exported, corrected, deleted, or restricted upon request?
Does your service support lawful response to access, deletion, correction, portability, objection, or restriction requests?
Rights support is partial with no clear limitations
Are end users informed when they are interacting with AI?
No notice when users interact with AI
Are customers provided with privacy disclosures, notices, or model usage explanations they can reuse?
Customers receive no transparency support materials
Does the system support output traceability or explainability for privacy-relevant outputs?
Audit trail or explainability feature evidence
If the service profiles individuals or supports decisions affecting them, what transparency controls exist?
Profiling or decision support exists without disclosure or challenge path

sections.heading: Section 9 — AI Training, Fine-Tuning, and Model Governance
tables: What sources were used to train the model(s)?
Training data source summary or model card
Were personal data or public internet sources included in training?
Personal data use in training is not disclosed
How was training data screened for privacy, consent, licensing, and appropriateness?
No screening for privacy, consent, or licensing
Are customer prompts, files, outputs, or telemetry used to fine-tune models?
Fine-tuning policy or data use setting
Customer content is used for fine-tuning without explicit authorization
Can customer-specific models or isolated fine-tunes be created?
Customer-specific isolation is unavailable for sensitive use cases
If yes, are those isolated from other customers?
Fine-tunes or models are not isolated
Are training, fine-tuning, or retrieval datasets reviewed for sensitive, inaccurate, or excessive personal data?
No review of privacy risk in datasets
Do you test for memorization, leakage, or regeneration of personal data?
Privacy leakage test results or summary
No testing for memorization or leakage
Do you monitor for model drift or changes that affect privacy risk?
Monitoring procedure or model governance docs
Are model updates communicated to customers before or after deployment?
Material updates are silent or non-transparent

sections.heading: Section 10 — Human Review, Annotation, and Access to Content
tables: Do employees, contractors, reviewers, or annotators access customer prompts, files, or outputs?
Human access exists without transparency or strict controls
If yes, for what purposes are human reviewers used?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Human access extends beyond necessary purposes
Human access is not role-based or logged
Is access restricted to approved personnel with confidentiality obligations?
Reviewers are not subject to confidentiality and screening
Are human reviewers located in specific countries or regions?
Can customers opt out of human review where not legally or operationally required?
No opt-out for discretionary human review
Describe monitoring and controls around human access to content
Monitoring evidence or admin oversight procedure
Human access monitoring is weak or undocumented

sections.heading: Section 11 — Security Controls Supporting Privacy
tables: Is customer data encrypted in transit and at rest?
No encryption at rest or in transit
Are tenant environments logically or physically segregated?
Tenant segregation is weak or unclear
Are access controls role-based and least-privilege?
Privileged access is broad or uncontrolled
Is MFA required for privileged access?
Are logs maintained for administrator access and sensitive actions?
Admin access or sensitive actions are not logged
Are vulnerability management and penetration testing performed?
Recent pen test summary or vuln management standard
Are secrets, credentials, and API keys securely managed?
Credentials or keys are not securely managed
Are embeddings, vector stores, and retrieval layers protected with access control?
RAG security design or access control evidence
Are prompts and outputs included in security logging?
No logging coverage for prompts/outputs where needed
Does the service support customer-managed keys, SSO, or regional isolation?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
No enterprise security features for sensitive deployments
Has the product experienced any privacy or security incident involving customer data in the last 24 months?
Recent material incident without clear remediation
If yes, describe the incident, impact, and remediation
Incident disclosure lacks scope, cause, or remediation detail

sections.heading: Section 12 — High-Risk and Regulated Use Cases
tables: Is the service intended for or commonly used in hiring, lending, insurance, education, healthcare, law enforcement, public sector, biometrics, children, critical infrastructure, legal advice, trading, or eligibility decisions?

Select all that apply

Vendor Response:
________________________
Use case statement or marketing materials
High-risk use case supported without sector-specific controls
Are there prohibited use cases defined by the vendor?
Acceptable use policy or prohibited use list
Are customers prevented technically or contractually from using the system in prohibited contexts?
Terms, abuse controls, or configuration evidence
Are additional safeguards required for high-risk or regulated deployments?
No enhanced safeguards for regulated use
Does the vendor provide documentation to support compliance reviews in regulated sectors?
Compliance pack, whitepaper, or evidence kit

sections.heading: Section 13 — Incident Response and Breach Notification
tables: Do you maintain a documented incident response plan covering privacy and AI-specific incidents?
No documented incident response for AI/privacy events
Does the plan address data leakage, model leakage, cross-tenant exposure, harmful output, prompt injection, unauthorized retention, and human review misuse?
IR scenario matrix or playbook details
What is your breach or incident notification timeline?
Notification timeline is absent or too slow
What is your process for customer communication during an incident?
Are affected customers provided with root cause analysis and remediation details?
Are incidents tracked and used to improve privacy controls?
No corrective action or control improvement loop

sections.heading: Section 14 — Contractual and Legal Commitments
tables: Will you sign a DPA or equivalent privacy agreement?
Vendor will not sign a DPA
Do you define your role as controller, processor, service provider, or another legally relevant role?
Role definition in contract or privacy terms
Legal role is undefined or inconsistent
Will you contractually commit not to use customer data for model training without explicit authorization?
No contractual restriction on training use
Will you disclose subprocessors and notify customers of changes?
Will you support audits, security reviews, or evidence requests?
No support for reasonable assurance requests
Do you offer contractual commitments for deletion, retention, region, and incident notification?
Core commitments are not contractually available
Are limitations, exclusions, or exceptions to privacy commitments documented clearly?
Material exceptions are hidden or ambiguous

sections.heading: Section 15 — Attestations and Evidence
tables: Please provide available privacy, AI, security, and compliance artifacts (privacy policy, whitepaper, DPA, subprocessors list, retention schedule, data flow diagram, AI governance policy, model card, SOC/ISO certificates, pen test summary, IR summary, PIA summary, residency documentation)
Vendor declines to provide basic supporting evidence
Has all information provided in this questionnaire been reviewed for accuracy?
Name, title, and signature of authorized respondent
Signed response cover page or e-signature

sections.heading: Appendix A — Risk Rating Legend
tables: No meaningful privacy risk identified. Evidence still should support the response.
Limited issue or low-relevance gap. Track if repeated across related controls.
Notable concern. Clarification, mitigation, or follow-up evidence needed.
Material concern. Escalate to business owner, privacy, legal, or security leadership.
Automatic escalation if unresolved. Usually blocks approval or requires executive exception.

title: GovSecure Third-Party AI Privacy Risk Assessment
